questions.question: Which loop construct guarantees the body runs at least once?
questions.explanation: do-while checks its condition after the first iteration, so the body executes at least once.

questions.question: What is printed? int sum = 0; for (int i = 1; i <= 3; i++) { sum += i; } System.out.println(sum);
Which option correctly prints each element of int[] arr = {2,4,6};?
options: B) for (int i = 1; i <= arr.length; i++) { System.out.println(arr[i]); }
C) for (int i = 0; i <= arr.length; i++) { System.out.println(arr[i]); }
D) for (int x in arr) { System.out.println(x); }
questions.explanation: Enhanced for iterates elements directly. B/C use invalid or out-of-bounds indexing; D is not Java syntax.

questions.question: Which header runs exactly 10 times and visits i values 0..9?
questions.explanation: Starting at 0 with condition i < 10 yields 10 iterations: 0 through 9.

questions.question: Given int i = 0;, which loop is infinite?
questions.explanation: for(;;) has no condition or updates and runs forever unless a break/return occurs.

questions.question: What does continue do inside a loop?
options: B) Skips the rest of the current iteration and checks the loop condition
D) Repeats the current iteration without rechecking the condition
questions.explanation: continue skips remaining body statements and proceeds to the next iteration's condition/update.

questions.question: What is printed? int count = 0; for (int i=0; i<5; i++) { if (i == 2) continue; if (i == 4) break; count++; } System.out.println(count);
questions.explanation: Increments when i=0,1,3 (skips 2, breaks at 4), so count is 3.

questions.question: How many asterisks are printed? for (int r=0; r<3; r++) { for (int c=0; c<4; c++) { System.out.print("*"); } }
Which is the correct Java syntax for a do-while loop?
questions.explanation: Java requires do { ... } while (condition); with a semicolon after the condition.

questions.question: What happens? for (int i=0; i<3; i++) { } System.out.println(i);
options: C) Compile-time error: i cannot be resolved
questions.explanation: i is scoped to the for loop header/body and not visible after the loop.
